Package microui.component

Class Button

java.lang.Object
microui.core.base.View
microui.core.base.SpatialView
microui.core.base.ContentView
microui.core.base.Component
microui.core.AbstractButton
microui.component.Button
All Implemented Interfaces:
Visible
Direct Known Subclasses:
MenuButton
public class Button extends AbstractButton
Represents a clickable button component with text and/or image content. The Button class extends AbstractButton to provide interactive button functionality with support for text rendering, image display, hover effects, and ripple animations. This component automatically handles text resizing and positioning within the button bounds.
See Also:
  • AbstractButton
  • TextView
  • ImageBuffer

  • Constructor Details

    • Button

      public Button(String text, float x, float y, float w, float h)
      Constructs a Button with specified text, position, and dimensions. The button is initialized with default styling from the current theme and includes automatic text resizing capabilities.
      Parameters:
      text - the text to display on the button
      x - the x-coordinate of the button's top-left corner
      y - the y-coordinate of the button's top-left corner
      w - the width of the button
      h - the height of the button

    • Button

      public Button(float x, float y, float w, float h)
      Constructs a Button with specified position and dimensions but no initial text.
      Parameters:
      x - the x-coordinate of the button's top-left corner
      y - the y-coordinate of the button's top-left corner
      w - the width of the button
      h - the height of the button

    • Button

      public Button(String text)
      Constructs a Button with specified text, centered on the screen.
      Parameters:
      text - the text to display on the button

    • Button

      public Button()
      Constructs a default Button with the text "BUTTON", centered on the screen. This is a convenience constructor for quick button creation.

      public final AbstractColor getRipplesColor()
      Gets the color of the ripple effects.
      Returns:
      the current ripple effect color

      public final AbstractButton setRipplesColor(AbstractColor color)
      Sets the color of the ripple effects.
      Parameters:
      color - the color to use for ripple effects
      Returns:
      this AbstractButton instance for method chaining

    • isRipplesEnabled

      public final boolean isRipplesEnabled()
      Checks if ripple effects are enabled.
      Returns:
      true if ripple effects are enabled, false otherwise

    • setRipplesEnabled

      public final AbstractButton setRipplesEnabled(boolean enabled)
      Enables or disables ripple effects.
      Parameters:
      enabled - true to enable ripple effects, false to disable
      Returns:
      this AbstractButton instance for method chaining

      public final AbstractButton setHoverSpeed(float speed)
      Sets the animation speed of the hover effect.

      speed - the animation speed (must be greater than 0)
      Returns:
      this AbstractButton instance for method chaining

    • getText

      public final String getText()
      Gets the text currently displayed on the button.
      Returns:
      the button's text content

    • setText

      public final Button setText(String text)
      Sets the text to display on the button. The text will be automatically resized and positioned according to the button's current dimensions and auto-resize settings.
      Parameters:
      text - the text to display
      Returns:
      this Button instance for method chaining

    • getFont

      public final processing.core.PFont getFont()
      Gets the font used for rendering the button's text.
      Returns:
      the current font, or null if using the default font

    • setFont

      public final Button setFont(processing.core.PFont font)
      Sets the font for rendering the button's text.
      Parameters:
      font - the font to use for text rendering
      Returns:
      this Button instance for method chaining

    • getTextColor

      public final AbstractColor getTextColor()
      Gets the color of the button's text.
      Returns:
      the current text color

    • setTextColor

      public final Button setTextColor(AbstractColor color)
      Sets the color of the button's text.
      Parameters:
      color - the color to use for text rendering
      Returns:
      this Button instance for method chaining

    • isTextVisible

      public final boolean isTextVisible()
      Checks whether the button's text is currently visible.
      Returns:
      true if text is visible, false otherwise

    • setTextVisible

      public final Button setTextVisible(boolean isVisible)
      Sets the visibility of the button's text. This can be used to hide text while keeping an image visible, or to create icon-only buttons.
      Parameters:
      isVisible - true to show text, false to hide it
      Returns:
      this Button instance for method chaining

    • getImage

      public final processing.core.PImage getImage()
      Gets the image displayed on the button.
      Returns:
      the current image, or null if no image is set

    • setImage

      public final Button setImage(processing.core.PImage image)
      Sets an image to display on the button.
      Parameters:
      image - the image to display on the button
      Returns:
      this Button instance for method chaining

    • getImageColor

      public final AbstractColor getImageColor()
      Gets the tint color applied to the button's image.
      Returns:
      the current image tint color

    • setImageColor

      public final Button setImageColor(AbstractColor color)
      Sets the tint color to apply to the button's image.
      Parameters:
      color - the tint color to apply to the image
      Returns:
      this Button instance for method chaining

    • setTextAlignX

      public final Button setTextAlignX(int alignX)
      Sets the horizontal alignment of the button's text. This determines how text is positioned within the button's bounds.
      Parameters:
      alignX - the horizontal alignment (LEFT, CENTER, RIGHT)
      Returns:
      this Button instance for method chaining

    • setTextAlignY

      public final Button setTextAlignY(int alignY)
      Sets the vertical alignment of the button's text. This determines how text is positioned within the button's bounds.
      Parameters:
      alignY - the vertical alignment (TOP, CENTER, BOTTOM)
      Returns:
      this Button instance for method chaining
